new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a distilled alcoholic beverage produced from a cereal mash, marketed in bottles or other containers at an alcoholic strength of 40% vol or more, qualifying it as whisky within CN heading 2208 30.
Distillation process applied to a cereal (grain) mash to produce whisky, yielding a spirit with an alcoholic strength of 40% vol or more as classified under CN 2208 30.
Quality borne by a whisky beverage to which soda water has been added, distinguishing it from plain whisky under CN 2208 30 61.

new_classes.label_en: Whisky with soda water, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2208 30 61)
new_classes.label_de: Whisky mit Sodawasser, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 30 61)
new_classes.definition_en: A distilled alcoholic beverage produced from cereal mash (whisky) to which soda water has been added, marketed at 40% vol or more, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 2208 30 61.

coverage_explanation: All principal discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) distillation from cereal mash → WhiskyGrainMashDistillationProcess + WhiskyQuality; (2) alcoholic strength ≥ 40% vol → decimalRange restriction on actualAlcoholicStrengthByVolumePercent; (3) soda water addition → SodaWaterAdditionQuality; (4) container ≤ 2 l → someValuesFrom SmallContainer. Minor gap: the maturation/ageing criterion mentioned specifically for Scotch whisky is not separately modelled for the general whisky category (it applies to Scotch specifically, not to this code which covers whisky+soda broadly). The distinction between Scotch and non-Scotch whisky origin is not required here because CN 2208 30 61 covers all whisky with soda water in small containers regardless of geographic origin. Coverage is therefore high but the Scotch-specific maturation nuance is not formalised, hence 0.92.
